title: Release Gates, Migration, Recovery, Support, and Defense · Runtime Lens: Recovery Rehearsal
summary: The team ships from generated volume, closes no playtest barriers, cannot identify an artifact, and treats reinstall as the recovery plan. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the recovery rehearsal increment in production release, rollback, and residual-risk defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Rehearse corrupted state, cancelled work, missing resources, display failure, bad release, rollback, and last-good restoration.
Compare behavior, saves, settings, key maps, artifacts, and accessibility across upgrades with rollback-safe migrations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 2173 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend release evidence, migrations, recovery rehearsals, support ownership, and residual risk; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Rehearse corrupted state, cancelled work, missing resources, display failure, bad release, rollback, and last-good restoration. A defensible solution applies Recovery Rehearsal, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to production release, rollback, and residual-risk defense.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-release-recovery-defense-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-release-recovery-defense-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_release_recovery_defense_2(release_gates: int = 12, passed_gates: int = 12, rehearsed_failures: int = 6, open_critical_barriers: int = 0, rollback_minutes: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if release_gates < 8 or passed_gates < 0:
        return False, passed_gates, "release-depth"
    if passed_gates != release_gates:
        return False, passed_gates, "failed-gate"
    if rehearsed_failures < 4 or open_critical_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_critical_barriers, "recovery-gap"
    if rollback_minutes < 0 or rollback_minutes > 15:
        return False, rollback_minutes, "rollback-budget"
    assert passed_gates == release_gates
    return True, passed_gates, "defensible-maze-release"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-recovery-rehearsal-1r6puz
# Competency: maze-recovery-rehearsal.
# Case 7539: a corrupted-state recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def evidence_maze_recovery_rehearsal_1r6puz(release_gates: int = 12, passed_gates: int = 12, rehearsed_failures: int = 6, open_critical_barriers: int = 0, rollback_minutes: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1r6puz = "1r6puz-zup6r1-7539"
    assert evidence_variant_1r6puz
    evidence_axes_1r6puz = (
        "grid1r6puz",
        "topology7539",
        "searchzup6r1",
        "event1r6puz7539",
        "access75391r6puz",
        "recoveryzup6r17539",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1r6puz)) == 6
    if release_gates < 8 or passed_gates < 0:
        return False, passed_gates, "release-depth"
    if passed_gates != release_gates:
        return False, passed_gates, "failed-gate"
    if rehearsed_failures < 4 or open_critical_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_critical_barriers, "recovery-gap"
    if rollback_minutes < 0 or rollback_minutes > 15:
        return False, rollback_minutes, "rollback-budget"
    assert passed_gates == release_gates
    return True, passed_gates, "defensible-maze-release"

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Rehearse corrupted state, cancelled work, missing resources, display failure, bad release, rollback, and last-good restoration.

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the recovery rehearsal defect evidence boundary.

For repair the recovery rehearsal defect, solve one convenient seeded maze, omit revision, coordinate and wall invariants, topology oracle, frontier trace, changed algorithm or heuristic, callback ownership, keyboard and structured-text access, native Tk evidence, failure, recovery, and package transfer, then accept a screenshot as maze proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Recovery Rehearsal, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Recovery Rehearsal model · Theory Recovery Rehearsal
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Recovery Rehearsal: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 7710 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend release evidence, migrations, recovery rehearsals, support ownership, and residual risk; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Rehearse corrupted state, cancelled work, missing resources, display failure, bad release, rollback, and last-good restoration.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Recovery Rehearsal model · Theory Recovery Rehearsal.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Recovery Rehearsal model · Theory Recovery Rehearsal, keep Recovery Rehearsal connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Rehearse corrupted state, cancelled work, missing resources, display failure, bad release, rollback, and last-good restoration.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Recovery Rehearsal model · Theory Recovery Rehearsal, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Recovery Rehearsal precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Recovery Rehearsal model · Theory Recovery Rehearsal, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 7710 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend release evidence, migrations, recovery rehearsals, support ownership, and residual risk; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Rehearse corrupted state, cancelled work, missing resources, display failure, bad release, rollback, and last-good restoration.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Recovery Rehearsal model · Theory Recovery Rehearsal, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Restarting the program is adequate recovery evidence.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-recovery-rehearsal-1r6puz(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_recovery_rehearsal_1r6puz\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?release_gates < 8 or passed_gates < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?passed_gates != release_gates)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?rehearsed_failures < 4 or open_critical_barriers > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?rollback_minutes < 0 or rollback_minutes > 15)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
